TensorFlow / Keras yfinance seaborn Weights & Biases Kaggle T4 GPU Grad-CAM
Motivation

During market stress, cross-asset correlations collapse toward +1 — equities co-move while bonds and gold decouple. This structural shift is invisible to price-level models yet visible as a distinct spatial pattern in the 30-day Pearson correlation matrix.

We render each matrix as a 64 × 64 heatmap image and train a CNN to classify regime from pixel structure alone — a pure vision approach to a structural finance problem.

BearVIX>30 OR drawdown>15%
BullVIX<20 AND drawdown<5%
NeutralResidual — everything else

CNN Architecture
Input 64×64×3
RGB heatmap PNG
↓
Conv2D(32) + BN
MaxPool → 32²
Conv2D(64) + BN
MaxPool → 16²
Conv2D(128) + BN
MaxPool → 8²
GlobalAvgPool → 128d
replaces Flatten
Dense(64) + Dropout(0.4)
Softmax(3)
bear / bull / neutral
Architecture Design Decisions
WHY GAP?
GlobalAvgPool prevents a 524k-param head on only ~3,400 training samples. Spatially averages feature maps instead of flattening.
WHY BATCH NORM?
Normalises activations per-batch, stabilises gradient flow through 3 conv layers, and acts as a mild regulariser.
WHY 3 CONV LAYERS?
Layer 1 detects edges/colour transitions; Layer 2 assembles block boundaries; Layer 3 captures full cluster patterns.
WHY DROPOUT(0.4)?
Applied to dense head only — prevents co-adaptation of 64 learned features without disrupting convolutional feature extraction.
Input — 30-day Rolling Correlation Heatmaps
Bear: uniform red equity block + blue TLT/GLD columns — crisis correlation spike. Bull: muted, mixed structure. Neutral: intermediate. Fixed vmin=−1, vmax=+1 encodes absolute magnitude as pixel intensity, enabling direct regime-to-regime comparison.
Class Imbalance — Problem → Fix → Residual Effect
Training set is 65% bull. Class weights (bear ×3.62, neutral ×1.27, bull ×0.52) scale the cross-entropy loss so rare-class errors penalise the network more. Right panel: despite the fix, the model still over-predicts bull on the test set, reflecting the bear class's genuinely ambiguous visual signature outside crisis periods.
Threshold Analysis — Per-Class F1 vs Bear Threshold
Bear F1 peaks near t=0.05–0.12 then declines steeply. Neutral collapses below t=0.12 — the bear threshold directly controls how much of the ambiguous neutral space is absorbed into bear predictions. t=0.10 is chosen as the operating point maximising bear recall while keeping macro-F1 above the argmax baseline.
Regime Timeline — Test Period 2021–2024
Top panel: ground-truth regimes shaded over rebased SPY. Bottom: model predictions at t=0.10. The 2022 bear cluster (Jun–Oct, –25% drawdown) is captured correctly. Most 2021 bear days are detected. The neutral class is near-absent in predictions — the low threshold converts borderline neutral days into bear calls.
Training Curves — Custom CNN vs MobileNetV2
Custom CNN: loss converges steadily; val macro-F1 peaks at 0.643 (epoch 15), early-stopped at epoch 22. MobileNetV2 (frozen ImageNet backbone): val macro-F1 plateaus at 0.605 by epoch 2 and stagnates — photographic low-level features do not transfer to numerical colour-encoded correlation matrices.
Model vs Baseline Comparison
ABLATION — WHAT EACH CHOICE CONTRIBUTES
Domain CNN learns correlation block structure — ImageNet features miss this entirely (+14 F1 vs MobileNetV2)
Class weights force bear learning; without them bear recall ≈ 0% (model predicts bull for everything)
Threshold t=0.10 recovers 51 pts of bear recall vs argmax without any retraining (+5 macro-F1 pts)
Fixed colour scale (vmin=−1, vmax=+1) is essential — adaptive scaling would erase the regime signal
Confusion Matrix (t = 0.10, n = 1,004 test days)
Left: raw counts. Right: row-normalised recall. Bear recall 61% (56/92), Bull 96%, Neutral 3%. The model overwhelmingly defaults uncertain inputs to bull — neutral has no sharp visual signature that separates it from low-volatility bull periods.

Head-to-Head
ModelVal Macro-F1Test Macro-F1
Custom CNN (argmax)0.6430.414
Custom CNN t=0.10 ★—0.463
MobileNetV2 (frozen)0.605~0.32

102k-param domain-specific CNN outperforms 2.3M-param ImageNet backbone by +14 macro-F1 pts. Smaller, faster, and purpose-built.

Key Finding
Bear recall: 9.8% → 61% via threshold calibration at t=0.10 — no retraining required. The network had learned a genuine bear signal; it was simply miscalibrated by the class imbalance. Lowering the decision boundary surfaces it. This is the central result: architecture was right, calibration was wrong.
Grad-CAM — Model Attention Maps
Two correctly-classified samples per regime. Bear: high attention on off-diagonal cells where TLT/GLD decouple — confirms the model learned genuine crisis structure. Bull: diffuse, low-magnitude. Neutral: scattered — no dominant correlation feature to attend to.
Precision-Recall Curves — Model Lift over Random Baseline
Shaded area = lift above random (dashed). Bear AP = 0.542 vs random = 0.092 → 5.9× lift. Bull AP = 0.809. Neutral AP = 0.422 barely clears random (0.329) — confirming weak separability in correlation space for the neutral class.
Key Findings
9.8%→61%
Bear recall via threshold calibration at t=0.10 — no retraining required
5.9× lift
Bear AP over random baseline — genuine learned signal confirmed
+14 F1
Custom CNN over MobileNetV2 — domain architecture beats ImageNet priors
3% recall
Neutral class — no sharp visual signature; AP only 0.09 above random
Why CNN for This Task?

Correlation matrices are structured 2D inputs — equity assets cluster top-left, macro instruments (TLT, GLD) separate bottom-right. A CNN's inductive bias (local receptive fields, translation equivariance) directly matches this: a 3×3 kernel detects a local correlation transition; stacked layers compose these into block-level regime patterns.

Alternatives (MLP, tree methods) require flattening the matrix, destroying the 2D spatial structure that carries the signal. Grad-CAM confirms the network exploits spatial structure — attending to specific off-diagonal regions, not the uninformative diagonal (which is always +1).

Limitations & Future Work

Neutral class: residual labelling gives no clean visual identity. A richer labelling scheme (late-bull / early-bear sub-regimes) would likely improve recall significantly.

Calibration gap: model trained on COVID-style crises (val 2019–20); 2022 slow structural bear required threshold recalibration. A multi-period validation set would yield better-calibrated probabilities.

Future directions: eigenvalue decomposition as side-channel · temporal smoothing prior · fine-tuning MobileNetV2 on domain data · regime-conditioned trading strategy overlay.

Reproducibility

RUN_FULL = False — load saved artifacts, end-to-end in ~30 sec on Kaggle T4

RUN_FULL = True — download prices, generate ~4,900 PNGs, train both models, ~25 min T4
